contrastMetadata.transferBoundary: Transfer when the output objective changes whether a retained entry can be finalized or proved permanently irrelevant before the scan ends.

authoringProvenance.author: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Pro — candidate authoring assistant; human review required

items.prompt: A design review covers Nested delimiters with mixed types. Explain why an unmatched opener remains on an ordinary stack. Which statement gives the decisive justification?
constraints: Use the exact output, strictness, scan direction, and tie semantics stated in the prompt.
Do not classify the algorithm from a variable name or one intermediate snapshot.

options.text: It is unresolved until the structurally corresponding closer arrives.
It remains because its value is not yet dominated.
Every older entry becomes obsolete as soon as a newer entry is pushed, so age alone justifies permanent deletion.
No retained entry may ever be deleted before the end of the scan, because future input can always make it relevant again.

feedback.reason: It is unresolved until the structurally corresponding closer arrives. This is the decisive boundary for the stated output and retained-state semantics.
feedback.details: The item is decided by the difference between pending state and a candidate that can be deleted permanently, not by a surface feature. It is unresolved until the structurally corresponding closer arrives. The tempting claim—It remains because its value is not yet dominated.—fails because it does not establish the semantic invariant that makes removal or retention safe. Transfer when the output objective changes whether a retained entry can be finalized or proved permanently irrelevant before the scan ends.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.target_false_heuristic: This is the targeted misconception. It is unresolved until the structurally corresponding closer arrives. The quoted clue does not establish that invariant or justify the removal rule.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.surface_feature_classifier: This relies on a surface feature instead of the contract. The correct decision follows from the difference between pending state and a candidate that can be deleted permanently; payload type or implementation shape alone is not diagnostic.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.unjustified_alternative_rule: This changes the representation or transition without proving that it preserves the requested result. It is unresolved until the structurally corresponding closer arrives.

items.prompt: Consider Iterative DFS or explicit recursion simulation. A teammate argues: “A newer frame permanently makes older work irrelevant.” Which response correctly resolves the design decision?
constraints: Use the exact output, strictness, scan direction, and tie semantics stated in the prompt.

options.text: A newer frame permanently makes older work irrelevant.
Every older entry becomes obsolete as soon as a newer entry is pushed, so age alone justifies permanent deletion.
No retained entry may ever be deleted before the end of the scan, because future input can always make it relevant again.
It represents deferred work to resume after more recent work completes.

feedback.reason: It represents deferred work to resume after more recent work completes. This is the decisive boundary for the stated output and retained-state semantics.
feedback.details: Start from the output contract and the meaning of each retained entry. It represents deferred work to resume after more recent work completes. That mechanism explains both the legal transition and the point at which an answer or pending state becomes final. The misconception “A newer frame permanently makes older work irrelevant.” confuses an implementation clue with a correctness condition. Transfer when the output objective changes whether a retained entry can be finalized or proved permanently irrelevant before the scan ends.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.unjustified_alternative_rule: This changes the representation or transition without proving that it preserves the requested result. It represents deferred work to resume after more recent work completes.

items.prompt: An editor performs edits A, B, C, then two undo commands. Order the state transitions and identify what remains reversible.
constraints: Order the steps by semantic dependency, not by the order in which they are listed.
The canonical scoring contract awards preserved adjacent relations.

elements.text: Pop and reverse edit C.
Pop and reverse edit B, leaving A as the latest still-reversible edit.
Push edit A into history.
Push edit B above A.
Push edit C above B.

feedback.reason: The sequence follows the dependency expressed by the decisive boundary: Entries persist as chronological history until explicitly consumed.
feedback.details: The stack category follows from the proof obligation. Entries persist as chronological history until explicitly consumed. Names, indices, comparisons, loop shape, or one trace snapshot cannot replace that proof. In this case, the stated misconception—Unpopped history entries are unresolved candidates for a greater value.—would permit transitions that are not justified by the requested result. Transfer when the output objective changes whether a retained entry can be finalized or proved permanently irrelevant before the scan ends. Reversing or separating a required adjacent relation either uses state before it is valid, loses provenance before writing an answer, or mutates the frontier before the invariant has been restored.

Every older entry becomes obsolete as soon as a newer entry is pushed, so age alone justifies permanent deletion.

Transfer when the output objective changes whether a retained entry can be finalized or proved permanently irrelevant before the scan ends.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.unjustified_alternative_rule: This changes the representation or transition without proving that it preserves the requested result. The current value is the nearest qualifying answer for the popped candidate.

constraints: Select all and only the statements that remain valid under the exact output and tie policy.
Each selected statement must be justified by retained-state semantics or a correctness/cost argument.

Whether equality satisfies the requested relation, such as greater-or-equal versus strictly greater.
Whether the output requires the nearest, earliest, or latest qualifying index.

Equal values always make the earlier candidate irrelevant.
